Title: Verify GET /users returns list of users successfully
Test Step: Step 1: Prepare a GET request to the endpoint '/users' with no payload.
Step 2: Send the GET request to the API.
Step 3: Validate that the response status code is 200.
Step 4: Verify that the response body contains a list of users represented as an array of user objects.
Step Expected: The API returns a 200 status code and a response body containing a list of users.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 400 when name is missing
Test Step: Step 1: Prepare a POST request to the endpoint '/users' with a payload containing only the 'email' field: {"email": "test@example.com"}.
Step 2: Send the POST request to the API.
Step 3: Validate that the response status code is 400.
Step 4: Check that the response body contains an appropriate error message (e.g., 'name is required').
Step Expected: The API returns a 400 status code and a response body indicating that the 'name' field is missing.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 400 when name and email are missing
Test Step: Step 1: Prepare a POST request to the endpoint '/users' with an empty payload: {}.
Step 2: Send the POST request to the API.
Step 3: Validate that the response status code is 400.
Step 4: Check that the response body contains an appropriate error message (e.g., 'name and email are required').
Step Expected: The API returns a 400 status code and a response body indicating that both 'name' and 'email' fields are required.

Title: Verify POST /users fails with 400 when format of email is incorrect
Test Step: Step 1: Prepare a POST request to the endpoint '/users' with a payload containing an invalid email: {"name": "John Doe", "email": "invalid-email"}.
Step 2: Send the POST request to the API.
Step 3: Validate that the response status code is 400.
Step 4: Check that the response body contains an appropriate error message indicating an invalid email format.
Step Expected: The API returns a 400 status code and a response body indicating the invalid email format.

Title: Verify POST /users handles case sensitivity correctly
Test Step: Step 1: Prepare a POST request to the endpoint '/users' with a payload: {"name": "John Doe", "email": "Test@Example.com"}.
Step 2: Send the first POST request to the API.
Step 3: Validate that the response status code is 201 (User created).
Step 4: Prepare another POST request to the endpoint '/users' with the same name but an email with a different case: {"name": "John Doe", "email": "test@example.com"}.
Step 5: Send the second POST request to the API.
Step 6: Validate that the response status code is 201 (User created) for the second request.
Step Expected: The API returns a 201 status code for both requests, treating email addresses with different cases as unique entries.
